reasoning: The document contains a critical programmatic defect where header/footer artifacts are retained across almost every page. Additionally, there are 6 major defects including list structure failures, significant visual duplication (both full-page and text block images duplicating extracted text), displaced hyperlinks, and truncated image alt text. These pervasive issues across a scanned document exceed the threshold for human review.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (17): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=17, pages_affected=16/17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

The conversion exhibits several significant list structure failures across pages 11 and 13. On page 11, item '2.' (under section I.B) completely loses its list numbering and is extracted as a top-level sibling to items A, B, and C instead of a child of B. Furthermore, the sub-items 'a.' through 'j.' under I.B.1 are flattened to the same hierarchy level as '1.' instead of being nested as children. On page 13, the list sequence fails to continue properly; items 'K.', 'L.', and 'M.' are extracted a
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Page 11: "2. If the maximum explosive weight per delay will vary..." 
Page 13: "K. Use of Permanent Seismographs. If permanent seismographs..."
concerns.output_quote:
Page 11 JSON drops the '2.' and elevates the item: `{"text": "If the maximum explosive weight per delay will vary during the shot..."}` (as a sibling to A, B, C). 
Page 13 JSON uses paragraph elements instead of list elements: `{"type": "paragraph", "text": "K. Use of Permanent Seismographs..."}`

concerns.analysis: The HTML output exhibits significant visual duplication where text blocks from the source document are presented as both structured HTML text and embedded screenshots of that exact same text. For example, the HTML contains the full text of Mark Piazza's email, immediately followed by an image whose alt text describes it as 'An email from Mark Piazza...'. Similarly, near the end of the Rep. Dennis Riddell email thread, the text for numbered items 4 and 5 is fully transcribed as an ordered list, b
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Pages 3, 5, and 7 contain standard email thread text and numbered lists.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML presents the text of these elements as proper text tags, but also embeds screenshot images of the text directly alongside them (e.g., and ).
concerns.location: Throughout the email chain bodies (corresponding to PDF pages 3, 5, and 7)

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (8 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=8. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 4933 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=4933. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 28 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=28, examples=['mailto:report.spam@nc.gov', 'http://www.colpipe.com', 'http://www.colpipe.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 11 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (11 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... featuring the state seal and listing Governor Roy"; p1: "...ormation to complete an application process. Below"; p2: "...ermit application deadline. It features a sign-off"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=11 (truncated=11, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF uses underlines for various subsection titles and list item headers (e.g., "Notice of Blasting Related Activities.", "Relocation of Emergency Access Roadway"). This underline emphasis is lost in the output, which renders them as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities. [Underlined in source]
concerns.output_quote: A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities. Alamance Aggregates shall provide...

action_items: Remove retained header and footer artifacts across all pages
Eliminate duplicate embedded screenshot images and full-page scans where text has already been extracted
Ensure hyperlinks are integrated inline within their text content
Complete the truncated alt text descriptions for meaningful images
